title: node:test, Fixtures, Compile-Negative Cases, Faults, and Mutation Evidence · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds risk-layered deterministic crawler test system from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of risk-layered deterministic crawler test system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, boundary, changed, Unicode, duplicate, inaccessible, and privacy-sensitive fixtures with provenance.
Test URL, robots, sitemap, state, scheduling, extraction, dedup, report, and recovery invariants with deterministic clocks and schedules.
Use expected TypeScript errors to prove invalid state, unsafe narrowing, mutable values, and missing exhaustive cases are rejected.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fixture Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9161 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove original fixtures, pure models, compile-negative tests, loopback faults, and mutation detection; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fixture Taxonomy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, boundary, changed, unicode, duplicate, inaccessible, and privacy-sensitive fixtures with provenance. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Fixture Taxonomy, keep Fixture Taxonomy connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
